Fix HBO in spectral_b and specsep fuzzers: outbuf missing nExtraSamples

commit.message:
Remove [DllImport("ext")] requirement for extern methods (#90)

The transpiler already detects extern methods via RelativeVirtualAddress == 0,
making the [DllImport("ext")] attribute unnecessary boilerplate. The "ext"
string was never read by the transpiler.

Now extern assembly functions can be declared with just:

  static extern void music_play(byte song);

instead of:

  [DllImport("ext")] static extern void music_play(byte song);

Changes:
- Suppress CS0626 in dotnes.props (allows static extern without [DllImport])
- Remove [DllImport("ext")] from climber and fami samples
- Remove using System.Runtime.InteropServices from samples
- Update RoslynTests to use plain static extern declarations
- Update docs to reflect the simpler syntax

commit.message:
Fix race condition: Droplet polls GitHub API for pipeline completion before running agent (#113)

* Initial plan

* Add run_daily_intel.sh: polls GitHub API for pipeline success before running Gradient agent

Fixes the race condition between the 8:00 AM UTC GitHub Actions pipeline
and the DigitalOcean Droplet's agent. The script uses the GitHub Actions
REST API to wait until today's daily_pipeline.yaml run reports success,
then runs git pull and python3 main.py.

Replaces the old blind 8:30 AM cron with an event-driven approach.

- Changed API query from status=success to status=completed (more defensive)
- Added T00:00:00Z suffix to date filter for proper ISO 8601 format

- Updated endpoint comment to clarify we check conclusion in the response body

commit.message:
Move Linux-specific symlinks from [base] to [linux] section (#263)

* Initial plan

* Fix Windows CI failure: move Linux-specific symlinks to [linux] section

Linux-specific symlinks (systemd, bash, zsh, tmux, ssh, etc.) were
in the [base] section of symlinks.toml, causing them to be installed
on Windows during CI. When uninstalling, Windows locked the .service
file (OS error 32) during rename, failing the uninstall step.

Fix: move all Linux-specific symlinks from [base] to a new [linux]
section (only active on Linux). Also add [linux] to manifest.toml so
sparse checkout excludes these files on Windows.

feat: Bracketed paste support (#219)

* feat: add bracketed paste foundation (Phase 1)

Add PasteContext (Channel<string>-backed streaming) and Hex1bPasteEvent
for detecting and streaming bracketed paste data.

- PasteContext: streaming paste context with ReadToEndAsync,
  ReadChunksAsync, ReadLinesAsync, CopyToAsync, SaveToFileAsync,
  cancellation support, and backpressure via bounded channel
- Hex1bPasteEvent: sealed record carrying PasteContext
- Enable bracketed paste mode (ESC[?2004h/l) in EnterTuiMode/ExitTuiMode
  for Hex1bAppWorkloadAdapter and InlineStepAdapter, gated on
  SupportsBracketedPaste capability
- Detect SpecialKeyToken(200/201) in DispatchTokensAsEventsAsync to
  stream paste content through PasteContext instead of individual
  key events
- Drain paste data on cancellation to prevent input stream corruption
- Add paste cleanup to Dispose/DisposeAsync
- 27 PasteContext unit tests + 18 PasteStreaming integration tests
- PasteDemo sample application

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add paste input routing (Phase 2)

Route Hex1bPasteEvent through InputRouter to focused nodes with
ancestor bubbling support.

- Add virtual HandlePasteAsync(Hex1bPasteEvent) to Hex1bNode base class
  (default returns NotHandled)
- Add Hex1bPasteEvent case to InputRouter.RouteInputAsync dispatching to
  new RoutePasteInputAsync method
- RoutePasteInputAsync: try focused node first, bubble up ancestors until
  Handled or all return NotHandled
- Hex1bApp.ProcessInputEventAsync: track active PasteContext, Escape key
  cancels active paste, auto-dispose unhandled paste contexts, clear
  tracking on paste completion via ContinueWith
- 9 paste routing tests covering: focused node handling, ancestor
  bubbling (single and multi-level), no handler, no focus, sequential
  pastes, early handling stops bubbling

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add TextBox paste handling (Phase 3)

TextBoxNode.HandlePasteAsync reads entire paste content and inserts
at cursor position as a single operation.

- Override HandlePasteAsync in TextBoxNode
- Replace newlines with spaces for single-line text box
- Replace selected text if selection is active
- Fire TextChangedAction callback with old/new text
- 9 TextBox paste tests: insert at cursor/start/end, replace selection,
  empty paste, multiline to single line, unicode, large text, callback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add PastableWidget container (Phase 4)

PastableWidget is a transparent container that intercepts bracketed
paste events for all descendants, with optional maxSize and timeout
enforcement.

- PastableWidget record with OnPaste(), WithMaxSize(), WithTimeout()
  fluent API
- PastableNode: transparent container (no visual chrome), overrides
  HandlePasteAsync to invoke paste handler with enforcement
- MaxSize enforcement polls TotalCharactersWritten (new property on
  PasteContext) and auto-cancels when exceeded
- Timeout enforcement via Task.Delay auto-cancel
- PastableExtensions: ctx.Pastable(child) and ctx.Pastable(builder)
- PasteContext.TotalCharactersWritten: thread-safe counter incremented
  on each write
- 13 tests: intercept, child priority, callback, chunks, lines,
  no handler, nested priority, maxSize cancel, timeout cancel,
  mid-stream cancel, character tracking, widget reconciliation,
  fluent API

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add OnPaste fluent methods for widgets (Phase 5)

TextBoxWidget.OnPaste() allows overriding the default paste behavior
(text insertion) with custom async handlers.

- Add OnPaste(Func<PasteContext, Task>) and OnPaste(Action<PasteContext>)
  to TextBoxWidget
- Add CustomPasteAction property to TextBoxNode
- Wire PasteHandler through reconciliation (TextBoxWidget → TextBoxNode)
- TextBoxNode.HandlePasteAsync checks CustomPasteAction first; if set,
  calls it instead of default text insertion
- 7 tests: override default, receive context, read, cancel, default
  fallback, fluent API, reconciliation wiring

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: update PasteDemo with all paste features

Showcase default TextBox paste, custom OnPaste handler (uppercase),
and Pastable container with streaming line display and MaxSize limit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: wrap PasteDemo pastable zone in Interactable for focus

The Pastable container is transparent (not focusable), so wrapping
its content in Interactable gives the zone a Tab stop, allowing
paste events to reach it via bubbling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: PasteDemo with streaming stats and live progress UI

Reworked demo to showcase massive paste handling with:
- Live character/line/chunk counters updated per chunk
- Throughput calculation (chars/sec)
- Elapsed time tracking
- Last 8 received lines displayed in real-time
- 15ms simulated processing delay per chunk
- Escape to cancel mid-stream
- Status transitions: idle → streaming → done/cancelled

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add animated spinner and periodic refresh during paste

Spinner (⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏) cycles every 80ms next to status and
inside the paste zone. A background task refreshes the UI at 80ms
intervals so the spinner animates smoothly between chunks, making
it visually obvious that processing is active.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: run paste handler off the render loop for live UI updates

Paste routing was awaited inline in ProcessInputEventAsync, blocking
the render loop for the entire duration of the paste handler. This
meant Invalidate() calls from the handler never triggered re-renders
until the paste completed.

Now the paste handler runs as a fire-and-forget Task.Run, so the
render loop continues processing invalidation signals and input
events (including Escape for cancellation) while paste data streams.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add volatile to _activePaste and guard Cancel() against disposed CTS

- Mark _activePaste as volatile for correct cross-thread visibility
  between the render loop and Task.Run paste handler
- Wrap _cts.Cancel() in try/catch for ObjectDisposedException to handle
  the race between Cancel() and DisposeAsync()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: improve paste API consistency

- Add PasteEventArgs for OnPaste handlers (consistent with other events)
- Make PasteContext constructor internal (users never create these)
- Make PastableNode properties internal set (reconciliation-managed)
- Rename ReadToEndAsync maxBytes → maxCharacters (it counts chars)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: increase WaitUntil timeouts from 2s to 5s for CI reliability

CI's 2-core runner has thread pool scheduling delays that cause app
startup to exceed the 2-second WaitUntil timeout, resulting in flaky
test failures. Increasing to 5s gives CI enough headroom while not
slowing down local test runs (WaitUntil returns as soon as the
condition is met).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: increase remaining WaitUntil timeouts from 1s to 5s for CI reliability

Same issue as the 2s timeouts — CI's 2-core runner can't reliably
render within 1 second. This fixes ProgressNodeTests and other
integration tests that used even shorter timeouts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: stabilize CaptureStep to wait for terminal output to drain

The CaptureStep now polls the terminal until two consecutive snapshots
have identical screen text, ensuring the output pump has fully processed
the render output before capturing. This eliminates race conditions
where WaitUntil succeeds on partial render output but the capture
snapshot is still missing content that hasn't been flushed yet.

Typically stabilizes in 1-2 polls (50-100ms overhead), with a 2.5s
worst-case ceiling. This fixes ~74 integration tests that were
vulnerable to flaky failures on CI's resource-constrained runner.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use Capture pattern in editor tests to avoid alternate-screen race

The Editor_TypeCharacter and Editor_TypeMultipleCharacters tests took
snapshots after await runTask, by which time the app had already exited
alternate screen and cleared the buffer. Add Capture steps before
Ctrl+C so the snapshot is taken while still in alternate screen.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Patch 068: Cap remaining OOM-inducing loops in fromxml/mluc paths

Cap 4 uncapped allocation loops causing OOM in icc_fromxml_fuzzer:
- ProfileSeqDesc XML parsing: 256 entries max
- ResponseCurveSet XML parsing: 64 curves, 8192 measurements max
- MultiProcessElement XML parsing: 256 elements max
- CIccTagMultiLocalizedUnicode::Read binary path: 4096 nNumRec max

Root cause: CIccLocalizedUnicode copy ctor at IccTagBasic.cpp:7123
allocating 659K copies (1.37GB, 44% of heap) from uncapped loops.

Extends patch 067 (which capped mluc XML path, ProfileSeqId, Dict).

CWE-789: Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value

commit.message:
fix: plug resource leaks in SNMP session setup and poll paths (#418)

Fixes #415

Four resource leaks in snmp.c and poller.c:

1. snmp.c: `session.peername` not `strdup()`'d, dangling reference after
`snmp_sess_open()`
2. snmp.c: `securityAuthProto`/`securityPrivProto` OIDs leaked on 4
error paths and happy path
3. snmp.c: PDU leaked in `snmp_get_base()` and `snmp_getnext()` when
`snmp_parse_oid()` fails
4. poller.c: `sprintf` overflow in `exec_poll()`, switched to `snprintf`

All declarations at block top per project convention. Tested with gcc
and clang.
